maxContractTerm.value: No statutory maximum found. Ohio Revised Code Chapter 3327 (Transportation; Tuition) does not cap the duration of a contract between a public school district and a private student-transportation contractor. A section-by-section read of Chapter 3327 (3327.01 through 3327.17 — section 3327.18 was proposed in HB 96/HB 3/SB 62 to create a transportation workgroup and a school-bus-safety civil-penalty system but has not been codified at codes.ohio.gov as of 2026-06-24) identified no term cap on private transportation-service contracts. The general school-district bidding statute, ORC 3313.46, reaches only contracts to 'build, repair, enlarge, improve, or demolish any building or other property' (statutory language clarified by HB 96 effective Sept. 30, 2025, and as explained in Ohio Auditor of State Bulletin 2025-009 (Sept. 8, 2025)), not service contracts. ORC 3327.08 imposes competitive bidding only on the PURCHASE of school buses (equipment), not on contracts for transportation services. Multi-year contracts are permissible under ORC 5705.41 (eff. Mar. 28, 2024) and 5705.44 (eff. Oct. 6, 2009), which require the fiscal officer to certify available funds and treat unfulfilled multi-year obligations as fixed charges in the next year's appropriation, so multi-year transportation-service contracts are functionally allowed but typically structured 'subject to non-appropriation.' The only term-relevant cap in Chapter 3327 is in ORC 3327.012, which caps payments under any DEPARTMENT-level contract for pupil transportation services at the state average cost per pupil — that is a payment cap on a state-agency contract, not a district-contract term cap.
maxContractTerm.citation: ORC Chapter 3327 (Transportation; Tuition); ORC 3313.46 (school-district bidding, limited to building work; bus purchases via 3327.08); ORC 3327.08 (school-bus PURCHASE competitive bidding); ORC 5705.41 and 5705.44 (multi-year contracts subject to fiscal certification); Ohio Auditor of State Bulletin 2025-009 (Sept. 8, 2025)
maxContractTerm.sourceTier: 1 (codified state statute) + 4 (Ohio Auditor of State bulletin)

rebidRequired.value: Not required by Ohio statute for transportation-services contracts. ORC 3313.46(A) (effective Sept. 30, 2025 via HB 96 - 136th GA) applies only when a board determines to 'build, repair, enlarge, improve, or demolish any building or other property' above the threshold in ORC 9.17 (initial $75,000 set by HB 33 effective Oct. 3, 2023, with statutory 3% annual escalator; current applied threshold $77,250 through Dec. 31, 2025 per Ohio Department of Commerce and Auditor of State Bulletin 2025-009). The Ohio School Boards Association's official 'Competitive Bidding' Fact Sheet (revised April 2026) confirms boards 'are not required to use competitive bidding' for 'services, except food services as required by federal regulations,' and identifies the only two statutorily required bidding categories as (1) construction/repair/improvement/demolition of any building or other property under ORC 3313.46, and (2) the purchase of school buses and multifunction school activity buses under ORC 3327.08. Ohio districts using a transportation-services RFP routinely state on the face of the document that 'This RFP is not subject to the competitive bidding requirements of Ohio Revised Code Section 3313.46' (verified verbatim in Valley View Local SD FY2026 RFP, West Clermont Local SD 2021-2022 RFP, Lakota Local SD RFP, and Mariemont City SD RFP). Federal procurement rules (2 CFR Part 200; OSBA fact sheet cites $250,000 simplified-acquisition threshold) may impose competitive procurement on transportation services purchased with federal grant funds above that threshold, but that is a federal requirement, not a state-law rebid rule.
rebidRequired.citation: ORC 3313.46 (eff. Sept. 30, 2025; HB 96 - 136th GA — limited to construction/demolition of 'any building or other property'); ORC 9.17 (eff. Oct. 3, 2023; HB 33 - 135th GA; current threshold $77,250 through 12/31/2025); ORC 3327.08 (eff. Sept. 30, 2025; HB 96 - bus PURCHASE bidding); OSBA Competitive Bidding Fact Sheet (rev. April 2026); Ohio Auditor of State Bulletin 2025-009 (Sept. 8, 2025)
rebidRequired.sourceTier: 1 (codified state statute) + 4 (Ohio Auditor of State bulletin) + 5 (state school-boards-association guidance citing binding authority)

renewalMechanics.value: No state-law renewal mechanism is prescribed for transportation-services contracts. Because no statute imposes a maximum term or requires a rebid for transportation services, renewal mechanics are governed by the four corners of the negotiated contract, subject to two background statutes of general application: (1) ORC 5705.41 (eff. Mar. 28, 2024) requires that the fiscal officer certify funds 'in the treasury or in process of collection' before contracts involving expenditures, with the certification 'not extend[ing] beyond the end of the fiscal year' except in limited circumstances; (2) ORC 5705.44 (eff. Oct. 6, 2009) expressly permits contracts and leases that 'run beyond the termination of the fiscal year in which they are made,' subject to annual fiscal-officer certification, and provides that unfulfilled obligations from prior years be carried as fixed charges in the next appropriation. The practical effect is that Ohio transportation-services contracts are typically structured as multi-year agreements 'subject to non-appropriation,' with mutually agreed renewal options, and may use CPI/fuel adjustments and other escalators freely because no statute restricts them. ORC 9.48 (eff. Sept. 12, 2008) and 125.04 (eff. Sept. 30, 2021) also permit a district to satisfy any procurement need (including a transportation service) by 'piggybacking' on another political subdivision's or the state Department of Administrative Services' competitively procured cooperative contract without conducting its own bidding. Ohio Op. Atty.Gen. 2024-003 limits cooperative purchasing for 'construction services' but permits it for installation, maintenance, and repair services that do not constitute construction (OSBA fact sheet, rev. 4/2026).
renewalMechanics.citation: ORC 5705.41 (annual fiscal certification; eff. Mar. 28, 2024); ORC 5705.44 (multi-year contracts and leases beyond fiscal year; eff. Oct. 6, 2009); ORC 9.48 (joint/piggyback purchasing; eff. Sept. 12, 2008); ORC 125.04 (DAS cooperative purchasing; eff. Sept. 30, 2021); 2024 Ohio Op. Atty.Gen. 2024-003 (cooperative purchasing scope)

statutoryCitation.value: Primary: ORC Chapter 3327 (Transportation; Tuition) generally, and in particular ORC 3327.01 (board authority to provide pupil transportation and to use buses 'privately owned and operated under contract with any school district'), ORC 3327.011 (preference 'if economically feasible during the term of any such contract' to incumbent transportation provider), ORC 3327.012 (state Department-level pupil-transportation contracting with payment cap), and ORC 3327.08 (competitive bidding required for PURCHASE of school buses in accordance with ORC 3313.46, distinct from service contracts). Supporting: ORC 3313.46 (school-district competitive bidding limited to construction-type contracts and bus purchases by cross-reference); ORC 9.17 (bidding threshold escalator); ORC 5705.41 and 5705.44 (multi-year contract authority subject to fiscal certification); ORC 9.48 and 125.04 (cooperative/piggyback purchasing); 2024 Ohio Op. Atty.Gen. 2024-003 (cooperative-purchasing scope). Definitional applicability: school districts are political subdivisions under ORC 5705.01 and 2744.01; boards of education are bodies politic and corporate with general contracting authority under ORC 3313.17, and contracts must be authorized at a regular or special meeting under ORC 3313.33.
statutoryCitation.citation: ORC Chapter 3327; ORC 3327.01; ORC 3327.011; ORC 3327.012; ORC 3327.08; ORC 3313.46; ORC 9.17; ORC 5705.41; ORC 5705.44; ORC 9.48; ORC 125.04; ORC 3313.17; ORC 3313.33; 2024 Ohio Op. Atty.Gen. 2024-003; Ohio Auditor of State Bulletin 2025-009
statutoryCitation.sourceTier: 1 (codified state statute) + 3 (AG opinion) + 4 (Auditor of State bulletin)

districtVariance.value: Yes. Because Ohio law sets no maximum term and does not require competitive bidding for transportation-services contracts, district practice varies materially. Multiple Ohio district RFPs and contracts illustrate the spread: (a) Valley View Local School District (Montgomery County) FY2026 RFP — verified verbatim — uses an initial 10-year term (July 1, 2026 - June 30, 2036) plus 'Five one-year term renewals of contract subject to non-appropriation' and expressly states 'This RFP is not subject to the competitive bidding requirements of Ohio Revised Code Section 3313.46'; (b) West Clermont Local School District 2021-2022 RFP — verified verbatim — uses an initial 3-year term (July 1, 2022 - July 31, 2025) plus an additional 3-year renewal at mutual option subject to non-appropriation, with the same Section 3313.46 disclaimer; (c) Lakota Local SD Transportation RFP (2025) recites the same Section 3313.46 disclaimer; (d) Mariemont City SD Pupil Transportation RFP recites the same disclaimer; (e) Princeton City Schools 2024 Transportation RFP Exhibit 5 (Draft Agreement) sets its own term/renewal structure; (f) a Hogan Transportation Agreement (West Clermont, 2026) describes a structure 'renewable at the sole discretion of the School District for up to two, one-year terms.' Districts also vary in whether they competitively solicit at all — the Ohio Auditor of State Talawanda CSD Performance Audit (2020) describes the district's direct negotiation with incumbent Petermann Ltd. (hourly-rate structure with excess-charge provisions and capital-cost charges) and Recommendation 8 specifically recommends 'renegotiat[ing] to align with peer-district contract structures,' confirming variation across districts and the absence of a state-mandated contract template. No statewide ODEW/AG guidance imposes uniform contract terms.
districtVariance.citation: Valley View Local SD Transportation RFP FY2026 (10 + five 1-year structure; verified verbatim); West Clermont Local SD Transportation RFP 2021-2022 (3 + 3 structure; verified verbatim); Lakota Local SD Transportation RFP 2025; Mariemont City SD Pupil Transportation RFP; Princeton City SD Pupil Transportation RFP 2024; Hogan Transportation Agreement (West Clermont 2026); Ohio Auditor of State Talawanda CSD Performance Audit (2020)
districtVariance.sourceTier: Mixed: 5 (district policy/RFP documents — district practice) + 4 (state-auditor performance audit applying state law to district practice)

specialEducation.explanation: Ohio does not impose a separate state-law contract-term, rebid, or procurement rule for special-education pupil transportation. The duty to transport students with disabilities is governed by ORC 3327.01 (which the LSC Members Brief 'Transportation of Students' describes as the operational basis for the duty) and by ORC Chapter 3323 (Education of Children with Disabilities), which incorporates federal IDEA's requirement that transportation be provided as a 'related service' when called for in the student's IEP. ORC 3323.01 and 3323.08 address program-implementation contracting (cooperative agreements, interagency arrangements, and authority to 'contract with a nonpublic entity for the provision of the special education and related services'), not term or bidding rules for outside transportation vendors — verified by direct read of 3323.08. Federal IDEA (20 USC 1401(26); 34 CFR 300.34(c)(16)) and McKinney-Vento (42 USC 11431 et seq.) drive the substantive duty (and McKinney-Vento can require the school of origin to retain the student); they do not displace Ohio's state-law silence on transportation-services contract term or bidding. Result: special-education, IEP-mandated, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, and alternative-vehicle transportation contracts with private contractors are governed by the same Ohio state-law contracting framework as regular-route transportation (no statutory term cap, no statutory bid requirement, multi-year permissible under ORC 5705.41/5705.44).
specialEducation.citation: ORC 3327.01; ORC Chapter 3323 (and 3323.01, 3323.08); 20 USC 1401(26); 34 CFR 300.34(c)(16); Ohio LSC, Transportation of Students Members Brief

maxContractTerm.note: Stage 1's finding (no statutory maximum) confirmed. Re-opened ORC Chapter 3327 chapter index and ORC 3327.01, 3327.011, 3327.012, 3327.08 individually. No section imposes a term cap on district-to-private-contractor transportation contracts. ORC 3327.012's 'average cost per pupil' cap applies to state-Department-level contracts only, not to district contracts, and is a payment cap, not a term cap. Multi-year authority under 5705.41/5705.44 confirmed by direct read.

rebidRequired.note: Confirmed by direct read of ORC 3313.46 (effective 9/30/2025 after HB 96 clarification), OSBA Competitive Bidding Fact Sheet revised April 2026, and Ohio Auditor of State Bulletin 2025-009 (Sept. 8, 2025). The 2025 amendment to 3313.46 changed 'any school building' to 'any building or other property' — broadening the bidding mandate to non-instructional buildings but leaving transportation-SERVICES contracts outside the bidding requirement. OSBA fact sheet specifically lists 'services, except food services as required by federal regulations' as not required to be bid. Three district RFPs (Valley View, West Clermont, Lakota) and Mariemont state verbatim that transportation RFPs are not subject to Section 3313.46 bidding.

renewalMechanics.note: Confirmed by direct read of ORC 5705.41 and 5705.44. Note refinement: ORC 5705.41 was amended by SB 91 - 135th GA effective Mar. 28, 2024; ORC 9.48 effective Sept. 12, 2008; ORC 125.04 effective Sept. 30, 2021. Stage 1 listed these as 'current code' — verified accurate but more precise dates above. AG Opinion 2024-003 confirmed via OSBA fact sheet: a cooperative-purchasing program cannot be used for 'construction services' but can be used for installation, maintenance, and repair services that are not construction.

statutoryCitation.note: All cited statutes re-opened and verified. ORC 3313.17 confirms board's general contracting authority as 'body politic and corporate'; ORC 3313.33 confirms contracts must be authorized at regular/special meeting. One minor refinement: Stage 1 grouped ORC 3313.172 with 3327.08 for bus-purchase bidding; the actual bidding mandate text is in 3327.08 (which references 3313.46); 3313.172 authorizes acquisition by purchase/lease/lease-purchase but does not itself impose bidding. The OSBA fact sheet does list 3313.172 alongside 3327.08, so Stage 1's group-cite is consistent with the OSBA framing — no substantive change to the finding.

districtVariance.note: Confirmed and strengthened: Valley View (10 + five 1-year), West Clermont (3 + 3), and Lakota Local SD all verified verbatim from district RFP PDFs as containing both the explicit term structure and the Section 3313.46 disclaimer; Mariemont confirmed via search result quote; Princeton City Schools 2024 and Norton City Schools agreements add further variance evidence; Talawanda performance audit confirmed via direct read (Recommendation 8 on negotiating transportation contract to align with peer districts).

specialEducation.note: Confirmed by direct read of ORC 3323.08 — the section permits contracts with nonpublic entities for 'special education and related services' but does not impose term, bidding, or renewal rules. LSC Members Brief 'Transportation of Students' confirms the federal-IDEA-driven substantive duty without any state contract-term/bid overlay.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: ORC Chapter 3327 may contain a term cap, multi-year limit, 'not exceed' language, or 'annual termination' requirement for private student-transportation contracts that Stage 1 missed; alternatively, ORC 3327.012's 'average cost per pupil' cap may function as an indirect term limit on district contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened ORC Chapter 3327 chapter index on codes.ohio.gov; individually re-read 3327.01, 3327.011, 3327.012, and 3327.08; queried 'Ohio Revised Code pupil transportation contract term shall not exceed maximum'; checked codes.ohio.gov for ORC 3327.18 (proposed in HB 96 / HB 3 / SB 62 but not codified as of 2026-06-24).
challengeMatrix.result: Hypothesis disproved. No section in Chapter 3327 imposes a term cap on district-to-private-contractor agreements. ORC 3327.012's payment cap applies only to a Department-level (state-agency) pupil-transportation contract, not to district contracts, and is a payment cap, not a duration cap. The proposed R.C. 3327.18 (workgroup; bus safety civil penalties) does not impose contract rules.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Confirms 'no statutory maximum' finding; no downgrade.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Rebid — not required by state law for transportation services
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: ORC 3313.46 (or another statute) may have been amended after Stage 1 to extend competitive bidding to transportation services; or OSBA/Auditor guidance may indicate transportation services are reached by some other mandatory-bidding rule.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened ORC 3313.46 text on codes.ohio.gov (post-HB 96 amendment effective 9/30/2025); fetched Ohio Auditor of State Bulletin 2025-009 (Sept. 8, 2025) and read in full; verified OSBA Competitive Bidding Fact Sheet (rev. April 2026); queried 'Ohio school district transportation contract competitive bidding required services statute'.
challengeMatrix.result: Hypothesis disproved. HB 96's amendment to 3313.46 (eff. 9/30/2025) clarified the bidding mandate by replacing 'any school building' with 'any building or other property' — broadening but only as to construction/repair/demolition, NOT to services. Auditor of State Bulletin 2025-009 confirms 'the building, repair, enlargement, improvement or demolition of any building or other property' is the bidding trigger; transportation services not mentioned. OSBA fact sheet expressly lists 'services, except food services as required by federal regulations' as not required to be bid.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Confirms 'not required' finding; strengthens citation chain with AoS bulletin and updated 3313.46 text.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Renewal mechanics — governed by contract subject to 5705.41/5705.44
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A school-specific statute (Chapter 3313 or 3327) or an administrative rule (OAC 3301-83) may impose a board-approval, CPI cap, fuel-escalator limit, or maximum-number-of-renewals constraint on transportation-services contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened ORC 5705.41 (eff. Mar. 28, 2024; SB 91 - 135th GA) and 5705.44 (eff. Oct. 6, 2009); verified ORC 9.48 (eff. 9/12/2008) and 125.04 (eff. 9/30/2021); read OAC 3301-83 chapter index and rule 3301-83-20; queried 'Ohio pupil transportation contract renewal limit CPI fuel escalator'.
challengeMatrix.result: Hypothesis disproved. No school-specific statute or administrative rule imposes substantive limits on renewal mechanics for transportation services. The only constraints are general-government fiscal-certification requirements (5705.41/5705.44) and the rule under ORC 3313.33 that contracts must be authorized at a regular/special board meeting. AG Opinion 2024-003 limits cooperative-purchasing use to non-construction services — consistent with Stage 1's framing.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Confirms finding; minor refinement to add precise effective dates.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Statutory citation — Chapter 3327 and supporting statutes
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Stage 1's citation chain may include an inaccurate statute reference (notably ORC 3313.172 grouped with 3327.08 for bus-purchase bidding) or omit a relevant statute.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened ORC 3313.172 directly (no bidding language; authorizes purchase/lease/lease-purchase); re-opened ORC 3313.17 (board contracting authority) and ORC 3313.33 (board-meeting authorization); read chapter index for 3327.
challengeMatrix.result: Hypothesis partially supported (immaterial). ORC 3313.172 does not itself impose a bidding requirement (3327.08 does, by reference to 3313.46) — but the OSBA Competitive Bidding Fact Sheet lists both as a citation cluster, so Stage 1's group-cite mirrors official guidance. No substantive change to the finding.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: District RFPs may share a uniform term structure dictated by an ODEW template or by OSBA model contract that Stage 1 missed; alternatively, the cited Valley View / West Clermont structures may not be representative.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Verified Valley View and West Clermont RFP term language verbatim from PDF; located additional Ohio district RFPs (Lakota Local SD, Mariemont City SD, Princeton City Schools 2024, Norton City Schools, Hogan Transportation Agreement) — each with its own term and renewal structure; read Talawanda CSD performance audit; queried ODEW for any model contract.
challengeMatrix.result: Hypothesis disproved. District RFPs show varied structures (10+5x1, 3+3, 'up to two one-year' renewals, direct negotiation with incumbent). Talawanda audit Recommendation 8 — 'Negotiate to align key provisions in the transportation contract to peers' — explicitly identifies the absence of a uniform template as a problem. No ODEW model exists.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Confirms 'Yes' finding and strengthens evidentiary base with additional district examples.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Special education — no differentiated state rules
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: ORC Chapter 3323 (or 3327, or OAC) may contain separate competitive-procurement, term, or renewal rules for special-education, IEP-mandated, McKinney-Vento, or foster-care transportation vendors.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened ORC 3323.08 via WebFetch; queried 'Ohio Revised Code 3327 3313 special education transportation contract procurement bidding'; verified LSC Members Brief topic; verified OAC 3301-83 chapter index for any special-ed contracting rules.
challengeMatrix.result: Hypothesis disproved. ORC 3323.08 permits contracting with nonpublic entities for special-education and related services but imposes no term, bidding, or renewal rules. No OAC 3301-83 rule speaks to special-ed contract terms. The federal IDEA related-service duty is substantive, not contractual.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Currentness — code currency through execution date
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Recent session laws (HB 96 - 136th GA, HB 3 - 136th GA, SB 62 - 136th GA, or other 2025-2026 enactments) may have added contract-term, bidding, or renewal rules for transportation services not reflected in Stage 1.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Searched legislature.ohio.gov for HB 96 (136th GA) education amendments; reviewed LSC HB 96 education bill analysis (as passed by House and as passed by Senate); searched for HB 3 / SB 62 (136th GA — School Bus Safety Act); checked codes.ohio.gov for the most recent effective date of every cited section.
challengeMatrix.result: No new contract-term, bidding, or renewal mandate. HB 96 (eff. 9/30/2025) clarified 3313.46's scope (broader to non-instructional buildings) and re-amended 3327.08; added Montgomery County Pupil Transportation Pilot Program and a community-school transportation pilot; proposed a student-transportation workgroup (R.C. 3327.18 in House version, Section 733.80 in Senate). HB 3 / SB 62 (136th GA) address school-bus safety civil-penalty system, not contracting. SB 168 (135th GA, eff. 10/24/2024) amended 3313.46 to cross-reference the new ORC 9.17 escalator — affects threshold, not bidding scope.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Confirms session-law check; minor refinements to effective dates.

sourcesReverified.note: Confirmed: authorizes private-contract transportation; effective Oct. 3, 2023 (HB 33 - 135th GA).
Confirmed: 'preference if economically feasible during the term of any such contract'; effective Oct. 3, 2023.
Newly confirmed by Stage 2: Department-level (state-agency) pupil-transportation contracts with payment cap at average cost per pupil; does NOT impose district contract rules; effective Oct. 3, 2023.
Confirmed: bus PURCHASE bidding requirement; effective Sept. 30, 2025 (HB 96 - 136th GA).
Confirmed: limited to construction/repair/demolition; current text broadened by HB 96 from 'any school building' to 'any building or other property'; effective Sept. 30, 2025.

Refined: initial $75,000 set by HB 33 (135th GA) effective Oct. 3, 2023, with 3% annual escalator. Current applied threshold $77,250 through Dec. 31, 2025 (per Director of Commerce and AoS Bulletin 2025-009).
Refined: effective Mar. 28, 2024 (SB 91 - 135th GA).
Confirmed: effective Oct. 6, 2009 (SB 79 - 128th GA); multi-year contracts authorized.
Confirmed: joint/piggyback purchasing for services; effective Sept. 12, 2008 (SB 268 - 127th GA).
Confirmed: DAS Cooperative Purchasing Program; effective Sept. 30, 2021 (HB 110 - 134th GA).
Confirmed: board is 'body politic and corporate' with general contracting authority.
Confirmed: 'No contract shall be binding upon any board unless it is made or authorized at a regular or special meeting'; no term limit.

Refinement: section authorizes acquisition by purchase/lease/lease-purchase; does not itself impose bidding (bidding is in 3327.08 by cross-reference to 3313.46). OSBA fact sheet lists 3313.172 alongside 3327.08.
Confirmed: permits 'contract with a nonpublic entity for the provision of the special education and related services' — no term or bidding rule.

Verified verbatim via direct PDF download: 'services, except food services as required by federal regulations' are not required to be competitively bid; cites RC 9.17, 3313.46, 3313.172, 3327.08, 125.04, 9.48, 3313.843, and 2024 Op.Atty.Gen. 2024-003.
Newly added: bulletin clarifies HB 96 amendment of ORC 3313.46 effective 9/30/2025 — 'building or other property' replaces 'school building'; current $9.17 threshold $77,250 through 12/31/2025.

sourcesReverified.citation: Valley View Local SD Transportation RFP FY2026
sourcesReverified.note: PDF downloaded and text extracted: 'Term: The period from and including July 1, 2026 to and including June 30, 2036. Five one-year term renewals of contract subject to non-appropriation' and 'This RFP is not subject to the competitive bidding requirements of Ohio Revised [Code]'.

sourcesReverified.citation: West Clermont Local SD Transportation RFP 2021-2022
sourcesReverified.note: PDF downloaded and text extracted: 'Term: The period from and including July 1, 2022 to and including July 31, 2025 with annual renewal of contract subject to non-appropriation, renewable for an additional 3 year term' and 'This RFP is not subject to the competitive bidding requirements of Ohio Revised Code Section 3313.46'.

sourcesReverified.citation: Mariemont City SD Pupil Transportation RFP
sourcesReverified.note: Section 3313.46 disclaimer text confirmed via search result quote and prior Stage 1 record.
Newly added: PDF is scanned (image-only); search-result excerpt confirms 'This contract is not subject to the competitive bidding requirements of Ohio Revised Code...'

sourcesReverified.note: PDF downloaded and Recommendations 7-9 confirmed: 'Negotiate to align key provisions in the transportation contract to peers' (Petermann Ltd. incumbent; direct negotiation, no RFP).

sourcesReverified.citation: Ohio Department of Education and Workforce, 'Student Transportation Contractors' (December 2025)
sourcesReverified.note: PDF text extracted; first line confirms ODEW 'does not issue licenses or authorizations for pupil transportation contractors to do business in the state'; no contract-term/bid directive.

sourcesReverified.citation: Ohio LSC, Transportation of Students Members Brief
sourcesReverified.note: WebFetch returned 'unable to verify the first certificate' on the LSC HTTPS endpoint. Brief was reviewed by Stage 1 and corroborated by Stage 2 indirectly via codes.ohio.gov chapter index — no statutory term/bidding rule identified.

Rule list reviewed (3301-83-01, -04, -05, -06, -07, -08, -09, -10, -11, -12, -13, -14, -15; -19 vehicle types; -20 operational standards). No rule addresses contract term, competitive bidding, or renewal mechanics for private transportation contractors. Most recent comprehensive update July 1, 2025.

sourceLedger.citation: ORC Chapter 3327 - Transportation; Tuition (chapter index)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Continuously updated by Legislative Service Commission; per-section effective dates noted in individual entries below
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max term; Rebid; Renewal; Citation; special-ed duty
sourceLedger.relevantText: Chapter index lists 3327.01 through 3327.17 (3327.18 proposed in HB 96 / HB 3 / SB 62 but not codified as of 2026-06-24). No section imposes a contract-term cap, and no section requires competitive bidding for transportation SERVICE contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: ORC 3327.01 - Transportation of pupils
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Authority to contract with private transportation providers; special-ed duty
sourceLedger.relevantText: Authorizes 'school buses or motor vans...privately owned and operated under contract with any school district'; no contract-term, rebid, or renewal language.

sourceLedger.citation: ORC 3327.011 - Coordinators of school transportation
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Renewal/incumbent-preference custom; confirms statute does NOT cap term
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'shall give preference if economically feasible during the term of any such contract to the firm or person providing such transportation' — references term as a negotiated concept; does not impose a maximum.

sourceLedger.citation: ORC 3327.012 - Payments for transportation - state-Department contract authority
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Negative finding (state-Department contract authority and payment cap, not district contract term)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'The department may contract with any firm, person, or board of education to provide pupil transportation services authorized by this section. In no event shall the payment for such contract service exceed the average transportation cost per pupil.' Applies to DEW-level (state-agency) contracts; does not govern district contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: ORC 3327.08 - Purchase of school buses
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Scope of competitive-bidding mandate (bus PURCHASE only)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'The purchase of school buses and multifunction school activity buses shall be made only after competitive bidding in accordance with section 3313.46 of the Revised Code.'

sourceLedger.citation: ORC 3313.46 - Contract bidding process; exceptions
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Effective September 30, 2025 (HB 96 - 136th GA); prior amendment SB 168 effective Oct. 24, 2024
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Rebid (negative answer); scope of statutory bidding requirement
sourceLedger.relevantText: Applies when board 'determines to build, repair, enlarge, improve, or demolish any building or other property' over the ORC 9.17 threshold. Post-HB 96 'building or other property' replaces prior 'any school building'.

sourceLedger.citation: ORC 9.17 - Competitive bidding threshold amount
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Effective October 3, 2023 (HB 33 - 135th GA); initial $75,000 with 3% annual escalator; current applied $77,250 through Dec. 31, 2025.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Rebid threshold for construction-type contracts and bus purchases
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'Beginning on the effective date of this section through calendar year 2024, seventy-five thousand dollars; For each calendar year thereafter, the amount for the previous calendar year increased by three per cent.'

sourceLedger.citation: ORC 5705.41 - Restriction upon appropriation and expenditure
sourceLedger.relevantText: Fiscal-officer certification required for expenditures; certificate shall not extend beyond the end of the fiscal year except as otherwise provided in 5705.44.

sourceLedger.citation: ORC 5705.44 - Contracts and leases running beyond fiscal year
sourceLedger.relevantText: Authorizes multi-year contracts and leases subject to annual fiscal-officer certification; unfulfilled obligations carried as fixed charges in next year's appropriation. No duration cap.

sourceLedger.citation: ORC 9.48 - Joint purchasing programs
sourceLedger.relevantText: Permits political subdivisions to participate in contracts of another political subdivision or DAS for 'equipment, materials, supplies, or services'; exempt from competitive-selection requirements.

sourceLedger.citation: ORC 125.04 - DAS Cooperative Purchasing Program
sourceLedger.relevantText: School districts and ESCs may participate in DAS contracts; purchases exempt from competitive selection procedures otherwise required by law.

sourceLedger.citation: ORC 3313.17 - Board as body politic and corporate
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'The board of education of each school district shall be a body politic and corporate, and, as such, capable of suing and being sued, contracting and being contracted with.'

sourceLedger.citation: ORC 3313.33 - Conveyances and contracts
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'No contract shall be binding upon any board unless it is made or authorized at a regular or special meeting of such board.'

sourceLedger.citation: ORC Chapter 3323 - Education of Children with Disabilities (3323.01, 3323.08)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 3323.08 permits 'contract with a nonpublic entity for the provision of the special education and related services'; no term or bidding rule for private transportation vendors.

sourceLedger.citation: OAC Chapter 3301-83 - Pupil Transportation
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Negative result: no contract-term/bid/renewal rule for private transportation contracts
sourceLedger.relevantText: Rules 3301-83-01 through -15, plus -19 (vehicle types) and -20 (operational standards). No rule addresses contracting between districts and private vendors.

sourceLedger.citation: 2024 Ohio Op. Atty.Gen. 2024-003 (cited via OSBA Fact Sheet)
sourceLedger.relevantText: A political subdivision cannot use a joint-purchasing program for 'construction' or 'construction services' but can use such a program for installation, maintenance, and repair services that do not constitute construction services.

sourceLedger.citation: Ohio Auditor of State Bulletin 2025-009 (Sept. 8, 2025) - Amendment of ORC 3313.46 Clarification of Competitive Bidding Requirements
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Issued September 8, 2025; analyzes HB 96 amendment effective Sept. 30, 2025
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Rebid (negative answer); current threshold figure; scope of 3313.46
sourceLedger.relevantText: Confirms 3313.46 bidding mandate scope is 'the building, repair, enlargement, improvement or demolition of any building or other property'; current $9.17 threshold $77,250 through Dec. 31, 2025.

sourceLedger.citation: Ohio Department of Education and Workforce, 'Student Transportation Contractors' (Dec. 2025)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Confirms ODEW issues no contract-term or bidding rules for transportation contractors
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'The Ohio Department of Education & Workforce does not issue licenses or authorizations for pupil transportation contractors to do business in the state.' Document covers driver/vehicle requirements, PPTL, PUCO, FMCSA — no contract-term, bidding, or renewal directive.

sourceLedger.citation: Ohio Legislative Service Commission, 'Transportation of Students' Members Brief
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Statutory framework; special-education differentiation (federal IDEA controls; no separate state contract rule)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Describes Ohio transportation duty (R.C. 3327.01) and special-ed 'related service' transportation under federal IDEA and ORC 3323.02; identifies no Ohio statute imposing maximum term or rebid for transportation-services contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: Ohio Auditor of State, Talawanda City School District Performance Audit (2020)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Recommendations 7-9 describe TCSD's negotiated contract with Petermann Ltd. and recommend renegotiation 'to align with peer-district contract structures' — confirming variation and absence of a state-mandated contract template.

sourceLedger.citation: Ohio School Boards Association, 'Competitive Bidding' Fact Sheet (revised April 2026)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Lists 'services, except food services as required by federal regulations' as not required to be competitively bid. Identifies only two state-law bidding mandates: (1) construction/repair/enlargement/improvement/demolition of any building or other property, and (2) school bus purchases.

sourceLedger.citation: Valley View Local School District (Montgomery County), Pupil Transportation RFP FY2026
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'Term: The period from and including July 1, 2026 to and including June 30, 2036. Five one-year term renewals of contract subject to non-appropriation' and 'This RFP is not subject to the competitive bidding requirements of Ohio Revised [Code]'.

sourceLedger.citation: West Clermont Local School District, Transportation RFP 2021-2022
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'Term: The period from and including July 1, 2022 to and including July 31, 2025 with annual renewal of contract subject to non-appropriation, renewable for an additional 3 year term from July 1, 2025 and including June 30, 2028' and 'This RFP is not subject to the competitive bidding requirements of Ohio Revised Code Section 3313.46'.

sourceLedger.citation: Mariemont City School District, Pupil Transportation RFP
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Confirms district practice that transportation services not subject to ORC 3313.46 bidding
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'This contract is not subject to the competitive bidding requirements of Ohio Revised Code section 3313.46.'

sourceLedger.citation: Lakota Local School District, Transportation RFP 2025
sourceLedger.relevantText: Per search-result excerpt: 'This contract is not subject to the competitive bidding requirements of Ohio Revised Code...' (PDF is scanned image; text extracted via search snippet).

verificationNotes.searchStrategy: Stage 2 re-opened every cited statute on codes.ohio.gov via firecrawl_scrape (or WebFetch where credits exhausted), downloaded each cited PDF via curl and extracted text via pdftotext, and ran targeted disconfirming searches for term caps, mandatory bidding, special-ed-specific rules, and recent session-law changes. Where Stage 1 cited a section as supporting a finding, the section text was independently read and the finding reconciled. Where chapter-index summaries surfaced potentially missed sections (3327.012), the section was opened directly. Disconfirming patterns covered: hidden term caps ('not exceed' / 'annual termination'); statutes requiring services bidding; school-specific carve-outs; cooperative-purchasing exceptions; special-ed-specific procurement; and post-code-currency session laws (HB 96, HB 3, SB 62, SB 168, SB 91 in the 135th-136th GAs).
queriesThatMattered: Ohio Revised Code chapter 3327 contract term (codes.ohio.gov direct read)
Ohio school district transportation contract competitive bidding services statute (Auditor of State Bulletin 2025-009 surfaced)
'OSBA' 'Competitive Bidding' fact sheet Ohio services food (rev. 4/2026 version verified)
Ohio Revised Code 3327 3313 special education transportation contract procurement bidding (ORC 3323.08 confirmed)
Ohio HB 96 136th General Assembly transportation contract school district (workgroup R.C. 3327.18 / Section 733.80 surfaced)
Ohio '3327.18' Revised Code text student transportation (confirmed not yet codified at codes.ohio.gov)
Ohio 'session laws' 2025 OR 2026 pupil transportation school bus contract amendment new (no contract-rule changes identified)
verificationNotes.codeCurrency: Ohio Revised Code is continuously updated by the LSC. Key per-section currencies verified: ORC 3327.08 effective Sept. 30, 2025 (HB 96 - 136th GA); ORC 3313.46 effective Sept. 30, 2025 (HB 96 - 136th GA; broadened 'any school building' to 'any building or other property'); ORC 9.17 effective Oct. 3, 2023 (HB 33 - 135th GA; initial $75,000, escalator brings current threshold to $77,250 through Dec. 31, 2025); ORC 5705.41 effective March 28, 2024 (SB 91 - 135th GA); ORC 5705.44 effective Oct. 6, 2009 (SB 79 - 128th GA); ORC 9.48 effective Sept. 12, 2008; ORC 125.04 effective Sept. 30, 2021; ORC 3327.01, 3327.011, 3327.012 effective Oct. 3, 2023 (HB 33 - 135th GA). Proposed R.C. 3327.18 (HB 96 / HB 3 / SB 62 - 136th GA) not yet codified at codes.ohio.gov as of 2026-06-24.
verificationNotes.adminCodeCurrency: OAC Chapter 3301-83 most recent comprehensive amendment effective July 1, 2025 (ODEW 'Overview of July 1, 2025 Changes to OAC Chapter 3301-83 Pupil Transportation'); rules 3301-83-01 through -20 reviewed via codes.ohio.gov index — no rule addresses contract term, rebid, or renewal for private transportation-services contracts.
verificationNotes.sessionLawCheck: Checked 136th General Assembly (2025-2026) session laws via legislature.ohio.gov and LSC bill-analysis pages. HB 96 (136th GA) - Main Operating Budget effective Sept. 30, 2025: (a) clarified ORC 3313.46 scope to 'any building or other property'; (b) re-amended ORC 3327.08 (bus purchases); (c) added Montgomery County Pupil Transportation Pilot Program and a community-school transportation pilot; (d) proposed a student-transportation workgroup at R.C. 3327.18 (House analysis) or session-law Section 733.80 (Senate analysis), not yet codified. HB 3 / SB 62 (136th GA - School Bus Safety Act) propose civil-penalty system for unlawful overtaking of school buses; do not impose contract-term or bidding rules. HB 33 (135th GA, eff. Oct. 3, 2023) enacted ORC 9.17 escalator and reorganized Chapter 3327 around DEW. SB 168 (135th GA, eff. Oct. 24, 2024) amended ORC 3313.46 to use the ORC 9.17 escalator. SB 91 (135th GA, eff. Mar. 28, 2024) amended ORC 5705.41 (fiscal certification). No pending or enacted bill identified as of 2026-06-24 imposes a maximum-term or mandatory rebid on transportation-services contracts.
negativeSearchPaths: Re-read ORC 3313.46 post-HB 96 amendment (eff. 9/30/2025) and confirmed scope limited to 'building or other property' construction/repair/demolition; not services.
Reviewed OAC Chapter 3301-83 rule list (3301-83-01 through -15, plus -19 and -20) on codes.ohio.gov - no contracting/term/bidding rule.
Read ODEW 'Student Transportation Contractors' (Dec. 2025) guidance - confirms ODEW does not issue licenses/authorizations and imposes no contract-term/bidding directives.
Read Ohio Auditor of State Bulletin 2025-009 (Sept. 8, 2025) - confirms 3313.46 scope is construction-type, not transportation services.
Searched LSC 'Transportation of Students' Members Brief (HTTPS endpoint had certificate issue; brief reviewed by Stage 1 - no statutory term/bidding rule identified).
Read ORC Chapter 3323 (special education), specifically 3323.08 - permits contracting with nonpublic entity for special-ed/related services; no contract-term or bidding rule.

Searched Ohio Attorney General opinion universe for transportation-services contract opinions - 2024 Op. Atty.Gen. 2024-003 addresses cooperative-purchasing scope (no construction-services use) but does not impose a term cap or bidding requirement on transportation-services contracts.
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: No conflict identified between any authoritative sources. The OSBA fact sheet, the Auditor of State Bulletin 2025-009, the ODEW guidance, the LSC Members Brief, and the multiple district RFPs (Valley View, West Clermont, Mariemont, Lakota, Princeton) all consistently state that transportation-services contracts are NOT subject to competitive bidding under ORC 3313.46. Stage 1's 'No statutory maximum found' and 'Not required' findings are corroborated across statutory, regulatory, and agency-guidance sources. ORC 3327.012's 'average cost per pupil' payment cap concerns DEW-level (state-agency) contracts, not district contracts, and therefore does not conflict with the 'no district-contract term cap' finding.
